messages.content: what if i need two admins? how to do that? its react native expo, ive already installed firebase and e.g.

To add two admins to your Firebase project in React Native Expo, you can follow these steps:

1. Go to the Firebase Console and select your project.
2. Click on the gear icon in the top left corner and select 'Project Settings'.
3. Click on the 'Users and permissions' tab.
4. Click on the 'Add Member' button.
5. Enter the email address of the first admin you want to add and select the appropriate role (e.g. 'Owner' or 'Editor').
6. Repeat step 5 for the second admin.
7. Click on the 'Send' button to send invitations to the new admins.

The new admins will receive an email invitation asking them to accept the role. Once they accept, they will have access to the Firebase project and its resources, including the database, authentication, and hosting features. Make sure to communicate with the new admins to establish their specific responsibilities and guidelines for managing the project.

how can i change language of messages when u reset password? 
Follow this link to reset your project-826298660199 password for 
i need also change the name of project to Белая Роза

To change the language of messages when resetting the password in your Firebase project and modify the project name, you can follow these steps:

1. Go to the Firebase Console and select your project.
2. In the sidebar, click on 'Authentication'.
3. Click on the 'Templates' tab.
4. Under the 'Email templates' section, click on the 'Change' button next to 'Password reset'.
5. In the 'Email body' section, you can modify the text of the email message to your desired language. Make sure to include the reset password link by clicking on the Insert link icon and selecting 'Password reset link'.
6. Click on the 'Save' button to save your changes.
7. To change the project name, go to the Firebase Console dashboard and click on the gear icon in the top left corner.
8. Select 'Project settings'.
9. In the 'General' tab, you can change the 'Project name' to 'Белая Роза'.
10. Click on the 'Save' button to save your changes.

With these steps, the password reset email message will be in your desired language and the project name will be 'Белая Роза'.
i need to do that admin of my project could add posts in the app. can i do it?

The error you are seeing might be caused by the `toDate()` function not working properly. You can try updating your code to handle the date object in a different way. Instead of using `.toDate()`, you can try using the `moment` library to handle the date formatting and manipulation.

Update your code to add `moment` library as a dependency:
```js
...
import moment from 'moment';
...

```

Then, replace this section of code:
```js
<Text style={gStyle.dateFutureMK}>{new Intl.DateTimeFormat('ru-RU',{
                day:'numeric',
                month:'long',
                year:'numeric',
                hour:'numeric',
                minute:'numeric',
                hour12:false
            }).format(mkData.time.toDate())}</Text>
```

With this one:
```js
<Text style={gStyle.dateFutureMK}>{moment(mkData.time.toDate()).format('D MMMM YYYY, HH:mm')}</Text>
```

This should format the date in `D MMMM YYYY, HH:mm` format, which you can customize as per your requirements. This should also resolve the error you are seeing.

If you continue to see errors after making this change, please provide more information about the specific error message and the line of code where it is happening.

Please provide more information on the error message you are experiencing so that I can better assist you in resolving the issue. Include the full error message and the line of code where the error is occurring.

If you are still seeing the same error even after adding the `futureMKData &&` check, it's possible that the issue is related to the `mkData` object not being properly set in your `Promo` component.

To debug this, you can add console logs to check the value of `futureMKData` and `mkData`, and see if they are not `undefined` or `null`:

```
console.log('futureMKData:', futureMKData);
console.log('mkData:', mkData);
```

If one of these console logs displays `undefined` or `null`, it could indicate that there is an issue with how your data is being fetched or stored. Check that your data fetching logic is working as expected and that the data is being properly formatted before being stored in the `futureMKData` state.

Also, make sure that you are correctly passing the `futureMKData` state as a prop to your `Promo` component, and that you are correctly accessing the `mkData` object within the `Promo` component.

If you are still experiencing issues, please provide the full error message and the code for your `Promo` component so that I can better assist you.

i deleted the new mk and now it doesnt have any errs

If you deleted the new MK that you added and your app is no longer throwing errors, it's possible that the issue was related to either the data formatting or storage, and not directly related to the code you wrote.

It's important to ensure that the data you add to your Firebase database is properly formatted and has all the required fields. It's also important to make sure that your code properly handles missing or incomplete data, in order to avoid errors.

When debugging Firebase errors, it's often helpful to log the returned data in the console and to review the Firebase documentation (including its error codes and troubleshooting guides) to get a deeper understanding of the problem.

If you encounter any future errors or challenges, I'm here to help!
